mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically elevating equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.5 percent signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of quarters where UNRATE stays above 8.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when tested out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A rising index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher short-term corporate cash flows and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns in >50% of weeks during late-cycle expansions, or the 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO growth falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 225.0 signals accelerating inflation that erodes real corporate margins and raises expectations for restrictive monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 225.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity that directly increases corporate earnings and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >60% of periods where GDPC1 growth is positive, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index below 90.0 indicates severe manufacturing contraction and underutilized capacity, which suppresses corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns in >50% of months where INDPRO stays below 90.0, or the threshold predictive power is statistically insignificant.
